items.title: Configuration and Setup
items.content:
# Module 1: Configuration and Setup

> **Exam weight: 15%** — this is one of the two heaviest sections on the ADM-201 exam, together with Object Manager & Lightning App Builder. Expect a large number of scenario-based questions here.

## 1. Company Settings

Every Salesforce org has a set of global settings configured under **Setup → Company Settings → Company Information**. As a Platform Administrator you must know what lives here and why it matters operationally:

- **Fiscal Year** — By default the fiscal year matches the calendar year (Jan–Dec). Under *Setup → Company Settings → Fiscal Year* you can define a **Standard Fiscal Year** (starts in any month, still uses standard quarters) or a **Custom Fiscal Year** (variable-length periods, e.g. 4-4-5 retail calendars). Custom Fiscal Year is irreversible once enabled and requires Salesforce Support to activate — remember this "cannot be undone" detail, it is a favorite exam trap.
- **Business Hours** — Defined under *Setup → Business Hours*. These are used by Entitlement Processes, Milestones, and Case escalation rules to calculate time-based automation (e.g., "escalate if no response within 4 business hours"). You can mark one Business Hours record as the organization default and activate/deactivate others per time zone.
- **Currency Management** — A single-currency org has one fixed corporate currency. Enabling **Multiple Currencies** (irreversible) lets you add currency types with conversion rates managed manually under *Setup → Manage Currencies*. **Advanced Currency Management** additionally supports dated exchange rates so opportunities use the exchange rate in effect on their close date instead of today's static rate.
- **Default settings** — Company Information also stores the org's default locale, language, and time zone, primary contact, org ID, and storage limits. These act as the *fallback* defaults applied to brand-new users unless overridden at the user record.

## 2. Declarative Configuration of the User Interface (UI/UX)

Administrators are expected to know every no-code lever for shaping what users see:

- **App Menu** — Configured under *Setup → App Manager*. Lightning Apps bundle a navigation bar, a set of tabs, branding (utility bar, logo, color), and a **User Profile/Permission Set visibility** setting that restricts which users see which app.
- **List Views** — Standard or custom, filterable by "All", "My", or custom criteria; can be filtered further with **List View filter logic**, made available **Kanban**-style, and shared with specific users, roles, or public groups (in Lightning). *Mass Update* via inline editing on a list view is a common exam scenario.
- **Global Actions vs. Object-Specific Quick Actions** — Global Actions (e.g., "Log a Call", "New Case") live in the Global Actions section of Setup and can create records not tied contextually to a specific parent, or log activities. Object-specific Quick Actions appear on a record's Lightning page and can pre-populate field values with **Predefined Field Values**.
- **Lightning App Builder** — The drag-and-drop tool for building Lightning Record Pages, App Pages, and Home Pages using standard/custom/Lightning Web Components. Activation controls (App, Record Type, Profile) decide which combination of users see a given custom Lightning page. This overlaps heavily with Module 2.

## 3. Setting Up and Maintaining Users

This is consistently one of the most tested areas of the whole exam.

**Uniqueness & format rules**
- The **Username** must be unique *across all Salesforce orgs globally* (not just your org) and is formatted like an email address, but does not have to be a real email. This is why "username already in use" errors can occur even in a brand-new org — someone else already claimed that exact string somewhere in the world.
- The **Email** field does not need to be unique and is where notifications/resets are sent.

**Locale, language, and working hours**
- **Locale** controls date, time, and number formatting (e.g., dd/mm/yyyy vs mm/dd/yyyy) and the first day of the week.
- **Language** controls the translated labels the user sees.
- Both can be set per-user, independent of the org default — a French-speaking user in a US org can have their own locale/language without affecting anyone else.

**Licenses**
- A **User License** (e.g., Salesforce, Salesforce Platform, Chatter Free, Chatter External, Experience Cloud licenses) determines the baseline set of features/objects a user can be granted access to, and is selected once when the user is created (generally cannot be changed after creation in most orgs without special handling).
- **Feature Licenses** (e.g., Marketing User, Knowledge User, Service Cloud User, Sales Console User) are *add-ons* layered on top of a User License to unlock specific features. They're assigned via checkboxes on the user record or through Permission Set Licenses.
- **Permission Set Licenses** allow you to grant access to a feature (like a specific Salesforce add-on product) without consuming a full user license.
- Distributing licenses: track usage under *Setup → Company Information* (shows licenses used/available) so you never attempt to create more users than your contract allows.

**Never delete a user**
- Salesforce does not allow hard-deletion of User records (data integrity — audit trails, record ownership history, and created/modified-by fields must always resolve to someone). Instead, admins **deactivate ("Freeze" or uncheck "Active")** users who leave the company.
- **Implications of deactivation**: a deactivated user's records remain, their name still appears as owner unless reassigned, they can no longer log in, and they stop consuming a license (freeing it up for someone else). Deactivated users can still be referenced in the role hierarchy, in fact you often need to keep them in the hierarchy briefly while records are reassigned. You cannot deactivate a user who is still selected as the sole "Case Escalation" or "Default Workflow User" owner elsewhere without first reassigning that role.

**Overrides & delegation**
- **Delegated Administration** lets you grant specific non-admin users limited rights to create/edit/reset-password/freeze users within selected roles/permission sets — without giving them the full System Administrator profile.
- Login access **override**: an admin can grant "Login As" access to troubleshoot as a user (subject to the org's "Administrators Can Log in as Any User" setting).

**Login methods**
- Users can authenticate via username/password, **Single Sign-On (SSO)** (SAML/OpenID Connect), **Social Sign-On**, or delegated authentication. Administrators manage multiple simultaneous login methods per org, and must understand **My Domain** is a prerequisite for most SSO and branding configurations.

## 4. Organization Security Controls

- **Identity Verification** — Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is required by Salesforce for all direct UI logins. Verification methods include the Salesforce Authenticator app, third-party TOTP apps, security keys, and built-in authenticators. As of recent policy, admins should always steer scenario answers toward enabling MFA rather than disabling it.
- **Setup Audit Trail** — A read-only log (retained for a limited rolling window, extendable/exportable) of configuration changes made by admins: profile edits, permission changes, workflow rule edits, etc. It does **not** log record data changes — for that, use **Field History Tracking** or **Field Audit Trail**.
- **Login Hours** — Set per profile; restrict the days/hours during which a profile's users may authenticate. If a user is logged in when their allowed window ends, they're not kicked out immediately but can no longer take further actions until the window reopens (in practice they lose access).
- **Session Settings** — Configured under *Setup → Session Settings*: session timeout value, "Lock sessions to the IP address from which they originated," "Force logout on session timeout," and Session security levels used for High Assurance requirements (e.g., requiring MFA step-up before exporting reports).
- **Agent/API access** — Controls like **Login IP Ranges** (per profile — logins outside the range require additional identity verification instead of being blocked outright, unless "Enforce login IP ranges on every request" applies at the org level), **Connected Apps** (govern OAuth access for external integrations/agents), and API-enabled permission gate machine access.

## 5. The Salesforce Sharing Model

The exam loves layered "who can see what" scenarios. Know the full toolkit and the order in which access is evaluated:

1. **Organization-Wide Defaults (OWD)** set the *baseline/most restrictive* access level per object: Private, Public Read Only, Public Read/Write (and Controlled by Parent for detail objects in master-detail relationships). OWD is the floor — everything else *adds* access on top of it, never removes it.
2. **Role Hierarchy** — Grants managers automatic visibility into the records owned by their subordinates (assuming "Grant Access Using Hierarchies" is checked for that object — this checkbox is only shown/relevant when OWD is Private or Public Read Only).
3. **Sharing Rules** — Automatically extend access to records beyond the role hierarchy/OWD, based on record **ownership** or **criteria**, targeting public groups, roles, or roles-and-subordinates.
4. **Public Groups** — Reusable bundles of users, roles, and/or other groups, used as the target of sharing rules and as folder/report access boundaries.
5. **Manual Sharing / Apex Managed Sharing** — One-off or programmatic sharing for exceptions the declarative model can't express.
6. **Teams** — **Account Teams**, **Opportunity Teams**, and **Case Teams** let a record owner explicitly add colleagues (with a chosen access level) to a *specific* record for as long as that person needs it, independent of role hierarchy.
7. **Report and Dashboard Folders** — Access to a *report* is separate from access to the *underlying records*: a user can see a report but if OWD blocks them from a given row, that row simply won't display totals/details (or the report returns fewer rows) even though the folder itself is shared with them.

**Golden exam rule**: sharing tools can only *grant* additional access on top of OWD; profiles/permission sets can restrict what *object and field* a user can interact with, but never below what OWD + sharing computed for record-level visibility on an object the user otherwise has access to.

## 6. Profiles vs. Permission Sets vs. Permission Set Groups

- A **Profile** is mandatory — every user has exactly one. It sets baseline Object/Field permissions, page layout assignment, record type assignment, login hours, login IP ranges, and app visibility. Best practice in modern orgs is to keep profiles minimal (often one profile per license type) and grant most access via Permission Sets.
- A **Permission Set** grants *additional* access on top of a profile — it can never take away access the profile already denies... actually it *can* extend beyond what the profile normally allows (permission sets are additive only; they cannot restrict). A user can have any number of permission sets.
- A **Permission Set Group** bundles several permission sets into a single assignable unit — useful for job functions that require many individual permission sets bundled together (e.g., "Senior Support Agent" = 5 different permission sets).
- **Muting Permission Sets** let you take a Permission Set Group and selectively "mute" (turn off) specific permissions granted by one of its member permission sets, without having to remove that member set from the group or edit the original permission set.
- **When to use a custom Profile vs. Permission Set**: use a profile difference only when the difference is about *login/session-level* settings (login hours, IP ranges) or when you need an entirely different record type/page layout default; otherwise, prefer permission sets/groups for functional access, since they scale far more cleanly across a growing user base and avoid "profile sprawl."

## Key takeaways for exam day

- OWD is the ceiling of restriction, not the ceiling of access — sharing tools only add.
- Never plan to delete a user; always deactivate and reassign ownership first.
- Username uniqueness is global, not org-specific.
- Multi-Factor Authentication is a mandatory Salesforce login requirement.
- Setup Audit Trail = configuration changes; Field History/Field Audit Trail = data changes.
- Permission Sets/Groups add access; Profiles set the mandatory baseline and login-level restrictions.

items.title: Quiz: Configuration and Setup

questions.question: The VP of sales at Cloud Kicks has a standard sales profile and is receiving an error message that prevents them from saving an opportunity. A Platform Administrator attempted the same edit without receiving an error. How should the administrator troubleshoot this issue?

options.text: Log in as a system administrator to troubleshoot.
Use 'Login as' to log in as the user.
Use an AppExchange product to troubleshoot.
Ask the user for their password so the admin can log in as the user.

questions.explanation: When a specific user encounters an error that an administrator cannot replicate, the most effective troubleshooting technique is to "Login as" that user. This feature, which must be enabled in the organization's login policies, allows the administrator to see exactly what the user sees and experience the system through their specific profile, role, and sharing permissions22. This is vital for identifying issues related to Validation Rules, Field-Level Security, or Record-Triggered Flows that might only trigger under specific user contexts. Logging in as a system administrator (Option A) is ineffective because administrators often bypass certain restrictions that apply to standard users4. Asking for a password (Option D) is a major security violation and is never required in Salesforce5. By using the "Login as" feature, the admin can pinpoint whether the error is caused by the user's data input or a specific permission conflict assigned to their profile.

questions.question: Ursa Major Solar has its business hours set from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM for the reps that are on Pacific Time. The reps on Eastern Time need business hours set to start 3 hours earlier to cover for support. How should a Platform Administrator solve for this issue?

options.text: Adjust the current business hours to accommodate the Eastern time zone.
Allow the reps to set business hours manually.
Set temporary business hours for each time zone.
Create one set of business hours per time zone.

questions.explanation: Salesforce allows for the creation of multiple Business Hours records to support global teams working in different time zones. To solve the requirement for Ursa Major Solar, the Platform Administrator should create two distinct sets of business hours: one for "Pacific Support" (9 AM - 5 PM PT) and one for "Eastern Support" (9 AM - 5 PM ET). This is essential because Business Hours are used by the system to calculate escalation rules and milestones correctly. For example, an escalation rule for an Eastern-based case 1should start counting at 9 AM ET, not 9 AM PT. Adjusting the current record to "accommodate" both (Option A) would result in a 12-hour window that doesn't accurately reflect either team's true availability. Users cannot manually set their own business hours (Option B) in a way that affects system automation. Creating one set per time zone ensures that the support team's performance metrics and automated routing are accurate and localized.

questions.question: Service reps in a call center do not have assigned desks. They sit at any available desk and use the computer on that desk to access Salesforce. A Platform Administrator has been asked to streamline the login process so the reps do not have to authenticate each time they log in at a different computer. Which function should the administrator use to implement this request?

options.text: Trusted IP Ranges IT Certification Guaranteed, The Easy Way! 7 Free Exam/Cram Practice Materials - Best Exam Practice Materials Get Latest & Valid Plat-Admn-201 Exam's Question and Answers from Freecram.net. 6 https://www.freecram.net/exam/Plat-Admn-201-salesforce-certified-platform-administrator-e17444.html

questions.explanation: In Salesforce, Trusted IP Ranges (configured under Network Access in Setup) define a set of IP addresses from which users can log in without being prompted for a verification code (identity confirmation). In a call center environment where reps use different machines but are all within the same corporate network (sharing a common external IP or range), adding those corporate IPs to the Trusted IP Ranges list "streamlines" the login process. This prevents the system from seeing each new machine as an "unrecognized device". Multi-factor Authentication (Option C) actually adds an extra step to the login process, which is the opposite of streamlining28. Profiles (Option A) and Permission Sets (Option D) can manage "Login IP Ranges" (which restrict access to specific IPs), but Trusted IP Ranges are the primary tool for bypassing identity verification within a known network.

questions.question: Universal Containers has two sales teams, sales team A and sales team B. Each team has their own role in the role hierarchy. Both roles are subordinates of the same Manager role. How should a Platform Administrator share records owned by sales team A with sales team B?
questions.explanation: In this scenario, the two teams are " peers " in the role hierarchy (both reporting to the same manager). In a private sharing model, peers cannot see each other ' s records by default. To grant - Best Exam Practice Materials lateral access between these specific groups, an Owner-based Sharing Rule is the most effective solution. The administrator can create a rule stating that any records owned by members of " Role: Sales Team A " should be shared with members of " Role: Sales Team B " with specific access levels (e.g., Read/Write). Hierarchical sharing (Option C) only grants upward visibility to the Manager role, not across to the other team34. Manual sharing (Option D) is inefficient for an entire team35. Criteria-based sharing (Option A) is used when record field values determine access, whereas this requirement is based specifically on who owns the records36.

questions.question: A Platform Administrator at Universal Containers is asked to restrict login access for users to specific hours and specific IP addresses to help minimize the risk of bad actors getting into the org. Which setting should the administrator update to accomplish this?
questions.explanation: In Salesforce, security restrictions like Login Hours and Login IP Ranges are managed at the Profile level. When these are configured on a profile, they are strictly enforced; if a user attempts to log in outside of the allowed hours or from an unauthorized IP address, the system will deny access entirely. This is one of the most robust ways to secure an organization against unauthorized access. Company Settings (Option A) can set " Trusted IP Ranges " for the whole org, but these only bypass multi-factor authentication; they do not restrict login. Roles (Option B) control record visibility, not system access. While Permission Sets (Option D) can grant many permissions, Login Hours and IP Ranges remain a core profile-level security setting.

questions.question: Which tool should an administrator use to identify and fix potential session vulnerabilities?
questions.explanation: Security Health Check is a tool that can be used to identify and fix potential session vulnerabilities. Security Health Check scans the security settings in an org and compares them to a baseline set of standards, such as the Salesforce Baseline Standard or the Salesforce Optimized Standard. Security Health Check provides a health check score and a list of issues and recommendations for improving the security settings. Reference:

questions.question: Clod Kicks has the organization wide defaults for Opportunity set to private. which two features should the administrator use to open up access to Opportunity records for sales users working on collaborative deals? Choose 2 answers
questions.explanation: Role hierarchy and sharing rules are two features that should be used to open up access to Opportunity records for sales users working on collaborative deals. Role hierarchy can be used to grant access to records owned by or shared with users who are below them in the hierarchy. Sharing rules can be used to extend sharing access to users in public groups, roles, or territories based on certain criteria, such as record owner or field value. Reference:

questions.question: Which setting on a profile makes a tab hidden in the All App Launcher or viable in arty app, but still allows a user to view records that would normally be found under this tab?
questions.explanation: To make a tab hidden in the All App Launcher or visible in any app, but still allow a user to view records that would normally be found under this tab, the administrator should use Tab Settings on a profile. Tab Settings control the visibility and default behavior of tabs for each app in an org. The administrator can set a tab to Hidden, which means it will not appear in any app or in the All App Launcher, but users can still access records via other means such as search or reports. Object Permissions, App Permissions, and Org-Wide Defaults are not related to tab visibility. Reference:

questions.question: An administrator installed a managed package that contains a permission set group. The permission set group that was installed includes Delete access on several objects, and the administrator needs to prevent users in the permission set group from being able to delete records. What should the administrator do to control Delete access?

options.text: Use a muting permission set with a permission set group to mute selected permissions.
Create a new permission set that has Delete access deselected for the objects.
Create a new role that prevents Delete permissions from rolling up to the users.
Edit the profile for the users to remove Delete access from the objects.

questions.explanation: Muting permission sets allow you to remove permissions that are granted by a permission set group.

questions.question: Cloud Kicks want its reports to show a Fiscal Year that starts on February 1 and has 12 months. How Should the Administrator Address this requirement?

options.text: Set the Fiscal Year to Custom and the starting month as February.
Set the Fiscal Year to Custom and the duration to 4 quarters.
Set the Fiscal Year to Standard and the starting month as February.
Set the Fiscal Year to Standard and the duration to 12 months.

questions.explanation: A standard fiscal year starts on January 1 and ends on December 31. A custom fiscal year can be set to start on any month and end on any month. In this case, Cloud Kicks wants its fiscal year to start on February 1 and end on January 31. This can be achieved by setting the fiscal year to Custom and the starting month to February. Setting the fiscal year to Standard and the starting month to February will not work, as the standard fiscal year starts on January 1. Setting the fiscal year to Custom and the duration to 4 quarters will not work, as the duration of a fiscal year is 12 months. Setting the fiscal year to Standard and the duration to 12 months will not work, as the standard fiscal year starts on January 1. Custom fiscal years are fiscal years that follow a custom-defined structure that differs from the Gregorian calendar. They can be used by organizations that have fiscal years that start on a different month than January or have fiscal years that are divided into custom periods such as quarters or weeks. To set up a custom fiscal year that starts on February 1 and has 12 months, an administrator needs to set the fiscal year to custom and the starting month as February in the fiscal year settings. Setting the fiscal year to standard or the duration to 4 quarters does not meet the requirement of having a custom fiscal year that starts on February 1. Reference: - Best Exam Practice Materials

questions.question: Universal Container wants to prevent its service team from accessing deal records. While service users are unable to access deal list views; they are able to find the deal records via a search. What options should the administrator adjust to fully restrict access?

options.text: Page layouts and field- level security

questions.explanation: Permissions and tab visibility are two options that administrators can adjust to fully restrict access to records for certain users or profiles. Permissions determine what users can do with records, such as create, read, edit, delete, view all, or modify all. Tab visibility determines whether users can see a specific object tab in their app launcher or navigation bar. By setting permissions and tab visibility to none or hidden for deal records for service users or profiles, administrators can prevent them from accessing deal records via search or other methods. References: htm&type=5

questions.question: Northern Trail Outfitters is using one profile for all of its marketing users, providing read-only access to the Campaign object. A few marketing users now require comprehensive edit access on Campaigns. How should an administrator fulfil this request?
questions.explanation: Permission sets are used to grant additional permissions and access settings to individual users without changing their profiles or requiring a new profile to be created. You can use permission sets to extend users' functional access without changing their existing profiles. To meet the request of giving comprehensive edit access on Campaigns to a few marketing users who have read-only access by default, you need to create a permission set with edit access on Campaigns and assign it to those users. References: com/s/articleView?id=sf.perm_sets_overview.htm&type=5

questions.question: The administrator for AW Computing is working with a user who is having trouble togging in to Salesforce. What should the administrator do to identify why the user Is unable to log in?

options.text: Review the login history for the user.
Check the attempted logins by running the setup audit trail.
Pull the password history to ensure the password policy was followed.
Reset the security token for the profile.

questions.explanation: To identify why a user is unable to log in to Salesforce, the administrator should review the login history for the user. The login history shows the date and time of each login attempt, the source IP address, the browser and platform used, the login type (such as username and password or single sign-on), and the status (such as success or failure). The login history can help troubleshoot common login issues such as incorrect username or password, invalid security token, IP restrictions, or login hours violations. Checking the attempted logins by running the setup audit trail, pulling the password history, or resetting the security token for the profile will not help identify why a user is unable to log in. References: monitoring_login_history.htm&type=5

questions.question: The IT manager at universal Containers is doing an audit of the systems security. Mow should the administrator provide a summary of the org's security health?

options.text: Change theOrganization-Wide Default to private to restrict visibility.
Turn on Event Monitoring to track user events.
Download the last six months of user login data.
Run a Health Check to identify vulnerabilities.

questions.explanation: To provide a summary of org's security health, an administrator should run a Health Check that compares org's settings against baseline settings defined by Salesforce Security Baseline Standard or industry standards such as CIS (Center for Internet Security) Benchmark Standard. Health Check generates an overall health score based on how org's settings match with baseline settings for various security categories such as Password Policies, Network Access, Session Settings etc. Health Check also provides recommendations for improving org's security health score by adjusting settings that do not match with baseline settings. Changing Org-Wide Default to private, turning on Event Monitoring, or downloading user login data will not provide a summary of org's security health. References:

questions.question: The Support team at Ursa Major Solar prefersusing split list views on the case homepage. Occasionally, the team views shipments from another support application. What should the administrator configure to allow the team to use the split list view?

options.text: Filter by a single shipment record type in the list view.
Include the Shipments tab on the app's navigation bar.
Split views are only available on standard objects.
Add the Manage List Views permission for support users.

questions.explanation: Split views are a feature that allows users to view records as asplit list on object home pages in Lightning Experience apps that use console navigation. Split views show records in two panes: a list view pane on the left and a record detail pane on the right. Users can switch between different list views and records without losing context or scrolling. However, split views are only available on standard objects such as accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, cases, etc., and not on custom objects such as shipments. References:

questions.question: Universal Containers has a private sharing model for Opportunities and uses Opportunity teams. Criteria-based sharing rules a sales rep at Universal Containers leaves the company and their user record is deactivated. The rep is later rehired in V administrator activates the old user record. The user is added to the same default Opportunity teams but h no longer able records the user worked on before leaving the company. What is the likely cause?

options.text: The stage of the Opportunity records was changed to closed lost.
Permission sets were removed when the user was deactivated.
The record type of the Opportunity records was changed.
The records were manual shared with the user.

questions.explanation: The likely cause for why a rehired user is no longer able to access records they worked on before leaving the company is that the records were manually shared with the user. Manual sharing allows granting access to individual records to specific users or groups. However, manual sharing is removed when a record owner changes or when a user's role changes. When a user is deactivated, their role is removed and any manual sharing involving that user is deleted. When a user is reactivated, their role is restored but manual sharing is not. Therefore, the rehired user will not have access to records that were manually shared with them before deactivation. The stage of Opportunity records, permission sets, or record type of Opportunity records are not likely causes for why a rehired user is no longer able to access records they worked on before leaving the company. References: id=sf.sharing_manual.htm&type=5

questions.question: The administrator at Northern Trail Outfitters has been using a spreadsheet to track assigned licenses and permission sets. What feature can be used to track this in Salesforce?
questions.explanation: To track assigned licenses and permission sets in Salesforce instead of using a spreadsheet, an administrator should use User Report type on Report object. User Report type allows creating reports that show information about users such as their profile, role, license type, active status, login history etc. It also allows adding fields related to permission sets such as Permission Set Assignments or Permission Set License Assignments. For example, an administrator can create a User Report that shows user name, profile name, user license name, permission set assignments count etc. Login History, Lightning Usage App, or Permission Set Groups are not features that can be used to track assigned licenses and permission sets in Salesforce. References: salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.reports_report_types_standard_user.htm&type=5

questions.question: How should a Platform Administrator view Currencies, Fiscal Year settings, and Business Hours in Salesforce?
questions.explanation: In the Salesforce Setup menu, Company Settings (formerly Company Profile) is the central location where global organizational parameters are managed. This section contains several key settings. Under Company Information, the admin can view the Org ID, default time zone, and primary currency. The Fiscal Year settings allow the admin to define whether the organization follows a standard Gregorian calendar or a custom fiscal cycle. Business Hours are used to define the working times for the organization, which is critical for calculating milestones in Service Cloud or escalation rules. If Multi-Currency is enabled, this is also where exchange rates and active currencies are managed. Viewing and configuring these settings is a foundational task for any Platform Administrator, as they establish the baseline for how data is interpreted and how time- based automation functions across the entire instance. Ensuring these are correct is vital for accurate financial reporting and maintaining service level agreements (SLAs).

questions.question: Northern trail Outfitter wants to use contract hierarchy in its or to display contact association. What should the administrator take into consideration regarding the contact hierarchy?

options.text: Contacts displays in the contact hierarchy are limited to record-level access by User.
Contact Hierarchy is limited to only 3,000 contacts at one time.
Customizing hierarchy columns changes the recently viewed Contacts list view.
Sharing setting are ignored by contacts displayed in the Contact Hierarchy.

questions.explanation: The contact hierarchy is a feature that allows users to view contacts related to an account in a hierarchical tree structure based on their role or position within the account. The contact hierarchy respects record-level access by user, meaning that users can see only those contacts that they have access to based on their profile permissions and sharing settings. The other options are incorrect because contact hierarchy is not limited to 3,000 contacts at one time (it can display up to 5,000 contacts), customizing hierarchy columns does not change the recently viewed contacts list view (it only affects how contacts are displayed in the hierarchy), and sharing settings are not ignored by contacts displayed in the contact hierarchy (they determine which contacts are visible to users). References: id=sf.contacts_hierarchy. htm&type=5

questions.question: Cloud Kicks (CK) has new administrator who is asked to put together a memo detailing salesforce uses to budget for upcoming license purchases. Where Should the administrator go to find out what type of licenses CK Has purchased and how many are available.

options.text: Search for licenses types in setup.
User Licenses Related List in Company information.
Usage based entitlement related list in company information.

questions.explanation: The User Licenses related list in Company Information shows the types of licenses that have been purchased for an org and how many are available orused. It also shows the expiration date of each license type if applicable. This information can help administrators plan for license purchases and manage user access. Searching for license types in setup does not show how many licenses have been purchased or how many are available or used. User Management settings in setup does not show license information either, but rather settings related to user login, session, identity, etc. Usage-based entitlement related list in company information shows information about usage-based licenses such as API requests or sandboxes, but not user licenses. References: id=sf.users_understanding_license_types.htm&type=5

questions.question: Once an opportunity reaches the negotiation stage at cloud kicks, The Amount fields becomes required for sales users. Sales managers need to be able to move opportunities inti this stage without knowing the amount. How should the administrator require this field during the negotiation stage for sales users but allow their managers to make changes?

options.text: Make the field required for all users.
Create to formula field to fill in the field formanagers.
Assign the administrator profile to the managers.
Configure a validation rule to meet the criteria.

questions.explanation: To require the Amount field during the negotiation stage for sales users but allow their managers to make changes, the administrator should configure a validation rule that checks if the user profile is not a sales manager, the stage is negotiation, and the amount is blank. This will prevent sales users from saving the record without entering an amount, but allow sales managers to doso. Making the field required for all users will not meet the requirement. Creating a formula field or assigning the administrator profile to the managers will not affect field requirement. References: id=sf.validation_rules_overview.htm&type=5

questions.question: Which tool should an administrator use to review recent configuration changes made in their org?
questions.explanation: Setup audit trail is a tool that allows administrators to review recent configuration changes made in their org. It shows a list of up to 180 days of setup changes made by anyone in the org, including the date, time, user, and type of change. It can help administrators track who made what changes and when, and troubleshoot any issues caused by configuration changes. Critical updates are notifications that inform administrators of new features or enhancements that may impact their org; they do not show configuration changes made by users. Debug logs are records of database operations, system processes, and errors that occur when executing a transaction or running unit tests; they do not show configuration changes made by users either. Field history tracking is a feature that allows administrators to track changes to the values of certain fields on records; it does not show configuration changes made in setup. References: id=sf.monitorsetup.htm&type=5

questions.question: A user at Universal Containers left the company. The administrator needs to create new user for their replacement, but they have assigned all available users licenses. What should the administrator do to free up users licenses for the new users?

options.text: Deactivate the former employees user record.
Change the formers users record to the new user.

questions.explanation: To free up user licenses for new users, the administrator should deactivate the former employees user record. This will prevent them from logging in and using Salesforce resources, but preserve their historical activities and data. Deleting or freezing user records will not release user licenses. items.title: Object Manager and Lightning App Builder
items.content:
# Module 2: Object Manager and Lightning App Builder

> **Exam weight: 15%** — tied for the highest weighting on the exam. This module is heavily scenario-based: "given a business requirement, which relationship/field/layout/record-type configuration solves it?"

## 1. Standard Object Architecture and Relationship Model

**Standard vs. Custom Objects**
- Standard objects (Account, Contact, Opportunity, Lead, Case, Campaign, etc.) ship pre-built with Salesforce and cannot be deleted, though many of their fields can be customized.
- Custom objects (API name ends in `__c`) are built by admins/developers for org-specific data.

**Relationship types — know the differences cold, this is tested constantly:**

| Relationship | Cardinality | Deletion behavior | Sharing | Required? | Reparenting |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Lookup** | 1:many (loose) | Deleting parent does NOT delete children (field is cleared unless "restrict deletion" is set) | Independent — child's sharing is not tied to parent | Optional | Always allowed |
| **Master-Detail** | 1:many (tight) | Deleting parent CASCADE-deletes all children | Child inherits parent's sharing ("Controlled by Parent") — child has no own OWD | Required — every child must have a parent | Only if "Allow reparenting" is enabled |
| **Many-to-Many (Junction Object)** | many:many | Achieved via a custom object with two Master-Detail relationships | — | — | — |
| **Hierarchical** | Self-referencing lookup (User object only) | — | — | — | — |

- A **junction object** is how you model many-to-many: e.g., a "Course Enrollment" object with a master-detail to Course and a master-detail to Student.
- **Roll-Up Summary fields** (COUNT, SUM, MIN, MAX) can only be created on the *parent* side of a Master-Detail relationship, and only summarize child records connected via that master-detail (this is why converting a lookup to master-detail is sometimes required specifically to unlock roll-up summaries).
- **Record Types** let a single object support multiple business processes, each with its own **picklist value sets** and (optionally) its own **page layout** assignment per profile. Record Types are also how the **Path** and **Sales/Support processes** differ per record type (e.g., separate Sales Processes drive separate Opportunity stage picklists per Record Type).
- **Schema Builder** is the visual, drag-and-drop tool for viewing and building the org's object model and relationships graphically — useful for both design and documentation.

## 2. Creating, Deleting, and Customizing Fields and Page Layouts

**Field creation considerations**
- Every custom field needs a **Field-Level Security (FLS)** setting per profile/permission set (Visible/Read-Only/Hidden) — a field can be on a page layout and still be invisible to a user if FLS blocks it. Page layout ≠ security.
- **Picklists**: single-select, multi-select, or **Global Value Sets** (a reusable list of values shared across multiple picklist fields on different objects, edited in one place).
- **Dependent Picklists**: a "controlling" field (picklist or checkbox) filters which values are available on a "dependent" picklist. Controlling fields cannot themselves be dependent on another field (no chaining beyond one level in the same relationship, though multiple independent dependent fields can share a controller).

**Implications of deleting fields — a frequent exam trap**
- Deleting a field moves it to the **Recycle Bin** for 15 days, during which it can be restored with its data intact; after 15 days it (and its historical data) is gone permanently.
- Deleting a field that is referenced in a **Roll-Up Summary** calculation, a **Cross-Object Formula**, an **Apex class/trigger**, a **flow**, a **validation rule**, a **report**, or a **picklist dependency** will either be blocked outright or will break/remove that dependency — Salesforce warns you and lists dependencies before allowing deletion in most cases, but formulas referencing the field will show `#ERROR!` if the deletion is forced through unexpected paths.
- Deleting a picklist value that's used as a *controlling* value in a dependent picklist relationship removes that dependency mapping.
- Deleting a **Master-Detail relationship field** is only possible after first deleting all child records or converting the relationship to a lookup (and only if there's no roll-up summary depending on it, which must be removed first too).

## 3. Page Layouts, Lightning Pages, Record Types, and Business Processes

**Page Layouts**
- Control which fields, related lists, buttons, and Quick Actions appear, and their read-only/required overrides *for a given Profile + Record Type combination* (Page Layout Assignment matrix).
- Note: Page Layouts cannot make a field visible if FLS hides it, and cannot make a field editable if FLS marks it read-only — FLS always wins as the more restrictive setting.

**Lightning Record Pages (via Lightning App Builder)**
- Unlike classic page layouts, Lightning pages are assigned via **Activation**: choose an **App**, **Record Type**, and **Profile** combination (the "App, Record Type, and Profile" assignment screen) so different audiences can see entirely different component arrangements even on the exact same record.
- Component visibility can be further filtered *within* a single Lightning page using **Dynamic Forms** (field-level visibility rules) and **Component Visibility Filters** (show/hide a whole component based on field values, device type, or user profile).
- The default org-wide fallback is the **"Org Default"** Lightning page if no more specific assignment matches.

**Business Processes**
- Sales Processes, Support Processes, and Lead Processes each define a subset+order of the master picklist values (Stage, Status) available to a given Record Type. Multiple business processes let, e.g., a "Renewal" Opportunity Record Type present a different Stage picklist than a "New Business" Record Type.

**Quick Actions and Global Actions**
- **Object-specific Quick Actions** (Create a Record, Log a Call, Update a Record, Send Email) live on that object's Lightning page/layout and can use **Predefined Field Values** to auto-populate hidden or default fields.
- **Global Actions** aren't tied to a specific object context; they typically appear in the utility bar or a global "+"/action menu and are used for things like universally logging a call or creating a case from anywhere in the app.

## Common exam scenario patterns to recognize

- *"Fields are missing from the page for a specific group of users but present for others"* → check page layout assignment (profile/record type) first, then FLS.
- *"A roll-up summary field option isn't available"* → the relationship is a Lookup, not Master-Detail; you may need to convert it (only possible if the child has ≤ a small number of related records limit and other prerequisites are met, and Master-Detail requires the field to not be blank on any existing record).
- *"Deleting a custom field breaks a report/formula"* → this is expected behavior; recommend checking field dependencies before deletion, or restoring from Recycle Bin within 15 days.
- *"Different users need to see a different layout/arrangement of the same record"* → Lightning App Builder activation matrix (App + Record Type + Profile), not just classic Page Layout assignment.
- *"A custom object can't have Tasks/Events related to it"* → enable **Allow Activities** on the custom object definition.

## Key takeaways for exam day

- Master-Detail = cascade delete + inherited sharing + required parent; Lookup = independent and optional.
- Roll-up summaries require Master-Detail on the parent side.
- FLS is always more restrictive than (and independent of) page layout field visibility.
- Lightning pages activate by App + Record Type + Profile; classic page layouts assign by Profile + Record Type.
- Deleting a field is recoverable for 15 days via the Recycle Bin, but breaks anything referencing it.

items.title: Quiz: Object Manager and Lightning App Builder

questions.question: What are three characteristics of a master-detail relationship?

options.text: Permissions for the detail record are set independently of the master.
The master object can be a standard or custom object.
Roll-up summaries are supported in master-detail relationships.
The owner field on the detail records is the owner of the master record.
Each object can have up to five master-detail relationships.

questions.explanation: A Master-Detail Relationship is a tight coupling between two objects that provides several unique functionalities: Master Object Types: The master can be either a standard object (like Account) or a custom object (Option B). Roll-up Summaries: This relationship type is the only one that natively supports Roll-up Summary fields, allowing the master record to calculate counts, sums, or averages of fields on the detail records (Option C). Ownership and Security: The detail record inherits its owner and its security/sharing settings from the master record. Consequently, there is no "Owner" field on a detail record; it is always owned by the owner of the master (Option D). Option A is incorrect because detail records cannot have independent permissions. Option E is incorrect because an object can only have a maximum of two master-detail relationships. This relationship is ideal for parent-child scenarios where the child's existence is entirely dependent on the parent.

questions.question: Cloud Kicks uses the standard Account Type field to indicate different account tiers. Users find this confusing, so management has asked that the field be changed to read "Tier" on the page layouts. How should a Platform Administrator implement this change?

options.text: Edit the Type field and change the name.
Build a custom field called Tier and delete Type.
Create a global picklist value set.

questions.explanation: To change the display name of a standard field (like "Account Type") globally across the entire organization, the correct tool is Rename Tabs and Labels in the Setup menu. This tool allows an administrator to modify the singular and plural labels for standard objects and the field labels for their standard fields. By renaming "Type" to "Tier," the change will be reflected on page layouts, in report column headers, and in list views. This is the preferred method because it preserves the underlying data and logic associated with the standard field. Option A is incorrect because standard field names cannot be edited in the "Fields and Relationships" menu. Option C is a destructive and complex process that would require data migration and could break existing reports or integrations. Option D does not address the label of the field itself. - Best Exam Practice Materials

questions.question: The Cloud Kicks sales team has asked that two of the fields that appear on the Opportunity cards in Kanban view be changed to make the cards more meaningful. Which feature should a Platform Administrator access to make this change?
questions.explanation: In the Salesforce Lightning Experience, the fields displayed in the " header " of a record and on the cards in the Kanban view are controlled by the Compact Layout. Each object has a System Default compact layout, but a Platform Administrator can create custom ones to prioritize the most important information, such as " Account Name " and " Close Date. " By editing the primary compact layout for the Opportunity object, the admin directly controls which fields the sales team sees as they drag and drop deals through the Kanban stages. While Page Layouts (Option C) control the main record detail page and Kanban Settings (Option D) control which field is used for columns and summaries, the individual " card " content is always driven by the Compact Layout. This ensures that users can quickly gather key context without needing to open every individual record.

questions.question: A user is unable to relate a task to the Course custom object. What should a Platform Administrator do to allow tasks to be related to courses?

options.text: Create a sharing rule for the Course object to grant the user Read/Write access.
Select Allow Activities on the Course object in Object Manager.
Add the Open Activities related list to the Course page layout.
Update the user's profile to grant them Edit access to the Task object.

questions.explanation: For a custom object to support Tasks and Events, the " Allow Activities " setting must be enabled in the object ' s properties. When a Platform Administrator creates a custom object, this checkbox is often left unchecked by default35. Enabling this feature allows the " Related To " (WhatId) field on a Task or Event to be linked to records of that custom object type36. While adding the related list to the page layout (Option C) is a necessary step for visibility, it will not work if the underlying feature isn ' t enabled first37. Sharing rules (Option A) and profile permissions (Option D) manage access to existing records but do not control whether the object is technically capable of having activities associated with it.

questions.question: Sales Users at Cloud Kicks are requesting that the data in the industry field on the Account object displays on the Opportunity page layout. Which type of the field should an administrator create to accomplish this?
questions.explanation: A cross object formula field is a type of formula field that references fields from related objects using relationships such as lookup or master-detail. It can be used to display data from one object on another object without creating another relationship or copying data. A cross object formula field can be created on opportunity object to display data from industry field on account object using account ID lookup relationship. A custom account field, a standard account field, or a master-detail relationship field are not types of fields that can display data from industry field on account object on opportunity page layout; they either do not exist or do not reference related objects. Reference:

questions.question: What are three characteristics of a master-detail relationship? Choose 3 answers

options.text: The master object can be a standard or custom object.
Permissions for the detail record are set independently of the master.
Each object can have up to five master-detail relationships.
Roll-up summaries are supported in master-detail relationships.
The owner field on the detail records is the owner of the master record.

questions.explanation: A master-detail relationship is a parent-child relationship in which the master object controls certain behaviors of the detail object. The master object can be a standard or custom object, but not all standard objects support being a master. Roll-up summaries are fields that calculate the sum, count, min, or max of child records. The owner field on the detail records is not available and is automatically set to the owner of the master record.

questions.question: Cloud kicks wants to track shoe designs by products. Shoe designs should be unable to be deleted, and there can be multiple design for one product across various stages. Which two steps should the administration configure to meet this requirement? Choose 2 answers

options.text: Create a Custom Object for shoe design.
Configure a Custom Lookup Field for shoe design on the product object.
Add a custom master detail field for shoe design on the Product Object.
Use the Standard Object for designs.

questions.explanation: Custom object and master detail field are two steps that should be configured to meet this requirement. Custom object can be used to create a new object for shoe design that can store information about different designs and stages. Master detail field can be used to create a relationship between Product and Shoe Design that prevents deletion of Shoe Design records and allows multiple designs for one product. Reference:

questions.question: What are two considerations an administrator should keep in mind when working with Salesforce objects? Choose 2 answers

options.text: Custom and standard objects have standard fields.
Standard objects are included with Salesforce.
A new standard object can be created.
Only standard objects support master-detail relationships.

questions.explanation: Standard objects are objects that are included with Salesforce by default, such as Account, Contact, Lead, Opportunity, etc. They have predefined fields and functionality that support common business processes. Custom objects are objects that you create to store information that is specific to your organization or industry. You can create new standard objects using the Object Manager in Setup. Reference: objects

questions.question: Cloud Kicks generates leads for its different product categories (shoes, apparel, and accessories) through many different sources. While some lead sources are used for all three categories, other lead sources are specific to a single category. The VP of marketing requests that only the proper lead sources be displayed based on the product category chosen. How should the administrator configure Salesforce to meet this requirement?

options.text: Create a page layout for each category and filter the Lead Source field based on category.
Create a dependency between the Product Category field and Lead Source field.
Create business processes and record types for each of the three product categories.
Create a single business process, then create record types for each product category.

questions.explanation: To display only the proper lead sources based on the product category chosen, an administrator should create a dependency between the Product Category field and Lead Source field on Lead object. A dependency is a relationship between two picklist fields that restricts the values available in one picklist based on the value selected in another picklist. For example, an administrator can create a dependency that shows only Online Store and Social Media as lead sources if Product Category is Shoes, but shows only Trade Show and Magazine as lead sources if Product Category is Apparel. Creating a page layout for each category, creating business processes and record types for each category, or creating a single business process with record types for each category will not display - Best Exam Practice Materials only the proper lead sources based on the product category chosen. Reference:

questions.question: Cloud Kicks is working on a better way to track its product shipments utilizing Salesforce. Which field type should an administrator use to capture coordinates?
questions.explanation: Geolocation fields allow you to store the latitude and longitude coordinates of a location. They can be used to calculate distances between records and display maps of accounts, contacts, leads, or other custom objects. References:

questions.question: Ursa Major Solar provides a 1-year warranty on all of the panels it installs. Installation details, along with the warranty information, a captured on a custom object called Installation. The installation record is created by the installer from the mobile app. Customers son receive a longer warranty as a way of increasing customer satisfaction when an installation gets delayed or has issues. How should the administrator configure Salesforce to capture the expiration date of the warranty?

options.text: Use a formula as the default value of the warranty Expiration Date field.
Create a formula field to display l year from the warranty purchased.
Add a validation rule to ensure the Expiration Date field is populated.
Include the warranty Expiration Date field on the mobile page layout.

questions.explanation: To capture the expiration date of warranty based on installation date and warranty length (1 year by default), the administrator should use a formula as the default value of Warranty Expiration Date field on Installation object. The formula can calculate one year from installation date using DATE function or DATEVALUE function. For example, DATE(YEAR(Installation_Date__c) + 1 , MONTH(Installation_Date__c) , DAY (Installation_Date__c)) will return one year from installation date. Creating a formula field, adding a validation rule, or including Warranty Expiration Date field on mobile page layout will not capture expiration date based on installation date and warranty length. References: id=sf.formula_using_date_datetime.htm&type=5

questions.question: The DreamHouse Realty team has a master-detail relationship set up with open house as the parent object and visitors as the child object. What type of field should the administrator add to the open house object to track number of visitors?
questions.explanation: A roll-up summary field is a type of field that calculates values from related records, such as the count of child records or the sum of a field on child records. In this case, the administrator can add a roll-up summary field to the open house object to track the number of visitors by counting the child records on the visitors object. References: id=sf.fields_about_roll_up_summary_fields. htm&type=5

questions.question: The administrator at Universal Container has created two objects: Containers_c Purchase_c, Management has requested that all container records display on purchase records in Salesforce. Which type of relationship between Containers_c and Purchase_c should satisfy the requirement?
questions.explanation: A lookup field is a type of field that allows administrators to create a relationship between two objects by linking records from one object to another object. For example, a lookup field can link an account record to a purchase record by storing the account ID on the purchase record. A lookup field allows users to select an existing record from a pop-up window or create a new record from the same window. References: salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.relationships_lookup.htm&type=5 Valid ADM-201 Dumps shared by ExamDiscuss.com for Helping Passing ADM-201 Exam! questions.question: Ursa Major Solar has a path on Case. The Company wants to require its users to follow the status values as they are on the path. Agents should be prohibited from preventing the case back to a previous status. Which Feature Should an administrator use to fulfill this request?
questions.explanation: Validation rules are a way to enforce data quality and business logic by preventing users from saving records that do not meet certain criteria. They can be used to require users tofollow the status values as they are on the path and prevent them from reverting the case back to a previous status by using formulas that compare the old and new values of the status field. Global value picklists are a way to create and maintain picklist values that can be shared across multiple fields, but they do not enforce any logic or order on the values. Predefined field values are a way to set default values for fields on path settings, but they do not prevent users from changing them later. Dependent picklists are a way to filter the values of one picklist based on the value of another picklist, but they do not prevent users from going back to a previous value.References: salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.validation_rules.htm&type=5 Valid ADM-201 Dumps shared by ExamDiscuss.com for Helping Passing ADM-201 Exam! questions.question: Sales and Customer Care at Ursa Major Solar need to see different fields on the Case related list from the Account record. Sales users want to see Case created date and statuswhile Customer Care would like to see owner, status, and contact. What should the administrator use to achieve this?
questions.explanation: Page layout editor is a tool that allows you to customize the layout and organization of detail and edit pages for a specific object and record type combination. You can also use page layout editor to customize related lists on detail pages by adding or removing fields, changing column order, sorting records, etc. To meet the requirement of showing different fields on the Case related list from the Account record for Sales and Customer Care users, you need to use page layout editor to modify the related list properties for each page layout assigned to those users. References: id=sf.customize_layoutrelatedlists.htm&type=5

questions.question: The Clientservices and customer support teams share the same profile but have different permission sets. The Custom Object Retention related list needs to be restricted to the client services teamon the Lightning record page layout. What should the administrator useto fulfil this request?
questions.explanation: Component visibility allows you to restrict the visibility of a related list based on a permission set. References: id=sf.dynamic_forms_component_visibility.htm&type=5

questions.question: The administrator at AW Consulting has created a custom picklist field. Business users have requested that it be a text field. The administrator attempts to change the field type but, is unable to because it is referenced by other functionalities. Which functionality is preventing the field type from being changed?
questions.explanation: Formula fields are types of fieldsthat calculate a value based on an expression or formula that references other fields or constants. Formula fields prevent administrators from changing their field type once they are created because they may be referenced by other functionalities such as reports, validation rules, workflow rules, etc., that depend on their data type and value. If a formula field is referenced by other functionalities, then changing its field type may cause errors or unexpected results. References:

questions.question: The administrator at Ursa Major Solar has been asked to change the work Item and Project Custom Object Relationship from a master detail to a Lookup. Which Scenario Could prevent theadministrator from fulfilling this requirement?

options.text: A junction object is required to support the lookup.
The lookup field in all the records contains a value.
The Look-Up field is required for Saving Records.
Roll-Up summary field sexist on the master object.

questions.explanation: One scenario that could prevent an administrator from changing the relationship between work item and project custom objects from master-detail to lookup is that roll-up summary fields exist on the master object (project). Roll-up summary fields are fields that calculate values from related records in a master-detail relationship; they cannot be used in a lookup relationship. If roll-up summary fields exist on the project object, they would prevent the administrator from changing the relationship type unless they are deleted first. A junction object is not required to support a lookup relationship; it is only used when creating many-to-many relationships between two objects using two master-detail relationships. The lookup field in allthe records containing a value or the lookup field being required for saving records are not scenarios that would prevent changing the relationship type; they are scenarios that would allow changing the relationship type without losing data or functionality. References: id=sf.fields_about_roll_up_summary_fields.htm&type=5

questions.question: An administrator needs to create a one-to-many relationship between two objects with limited access to child records. What type of field should the administrator use?
questions.explanation: A lookup field is a type of field that creates a relationship between two objects and allows users to select a record from one object as a value for another object. A lookup relationship creates a one-to-many relationship between two objects, where each parent record can have many child records but each child record can have only one parent record. A lookup relationship also allows limited access to child records, meaning that users can see only those child records that they have access to based on their profile permissions and sharing settings. References:

questions.question: The administrator at Aw Computing wants Account Details, related list and chatter feeds to each appear on separate tabs when reviewing an account. Which type of page should the administrator create?
questions.explanation: Lightning record page is a type of page that should be created to meet this requirement. Lightning record page allows an administrator to customize the layout and components for a specific object record page in Lightning Experience or the Salesforce mobile app. To create tabs for account details, related lists, and chatter feeds, use the Tabs component in the Lightning App Builder and drag the desired components to each tab. References: com/s/articleView?id=sf.app_builder_tabs.htm&type=5

questions.question: Users at Cloud Kicks are reporting different options when uploading a custom picklist on the Opportunity object based on the kind of opportunity. Where Should an administrator update the option in the picklist?
questions.explanation: Record types allow you to update the options in a picklist based on the kind of opportunity. References:

questions.question: Cloud Kicks (CK) stores information about specific customers in Contacts and information about shoes and accessories in a custom Merchandise object. What should the CK administrator use to represent that Contact can be interested in multiple pieces of Merchandies?
questions.explanation: A junction object is a type of custom object that allows administrators to create many-to-many relationships between two other objects. A many-to-many relationship means that each record of one object can be related to multiple records of another object, and vice versa. For example, a junction object can represent that a contact can be interested in multiple pieces of merchandise, and a piece of merchandise can be of interest to multiple contacts. A junction object has two master-detail relationships with the two objects it connects. References: id=sf.relationships_manytomany.htm&type=5

# Module 3: Sales and Marketing Applications

> **Exam weight: 10%**

## 1. The Sales Process: Leads, Opportunities, and Path

**Leads**
- A Lead represents an unqualified prospect (person + company data in one record). Leads have their own **Lead Status** picklist and their own **Lead Process** (which subset/order of statuses applies to a given Lead Record Type).
- **Lead Conversion** turns a Lead into an **Account**, **Contact**, and (optionally) an **Opportunity**. During conversion:
  - Standard fields map automatically.
  - **Custom fields must be explicitly mapped** using *Setup → Object Manager → Lead → Fields & Relationships → Map Lead Fields* — this does not happen automatically, and is a very common exam scenario ("custom lead field values disappear after conversion").
  - You can choose not to create an Opportunity at conversion (useful if the deal isn't ready to be tracked yet).
  - Conversion is permanent — a converted Lead cannot be "un-converted," though the original Lead record is retained (marked Converted) for audit/history purposes.

**Opportunities**
- Represent a specific deal in progress. Driven by **Sales Path** (Stage-based guidance banner showing key fields and optional Path guidance text per stage) and the **Sales Process** picklist (Stage values, each mapped to a Forecast Category and a probability %).
- **Opportunity Teams**: individuals added to a specific deal (with an access level and role) independent of the role hierarchy, useful for cross-functional deal support.
- **Big Deal Alerts** notify Chatter followers when a large opportunity closes or reaches a milestone value.

## 2. Sales Productivity Features

- **Dashboards** — visual summaries of report data; sales managers commonly use opportunity pipeline dashboards filtered by owner/team/stage.
- **Lead Scoring / Opportunity Scoring (Einstein)** — Einstein automatically evaluates historical won/lost patterns and assigns a predictive score to leads and opportunities, helping reps prioritize where to spend time. This requires Sales Cloud Einstein and sufficient historical data to train the model.
- **Einstein for Sales** (an umbrella of generative + predictive features) includes tools such as **Deal Change Highlights** (colored arrows/badges in list views and Kanban view showing recent Amount/Close Date changes for 7 days), **Einstein Activity Capture** (auto-logs emails/events from connected calendar/email), **Conversation Insights**, and **Relationship Intelligence**.
- **Grading** in the context of Lead Scoring can refer to letter-grade assessments (A–D) of lead fit based on firmographic criteria, distinct from the numeric behavioral "score."
- **Home Page Assistant** — a component on the Lightning Sales Console home page that surfaces AI-recommended next-best-actions (follow-ups, stale deals, etc.) for a rep's day.
- **Forecasting** — lets sales leadership project revenue across upcoming periods using Forecast Categories (Pipeline, Best Case, Commit, Omitted, Closed) derived from Opportunity Stage; supports **collaborative forecasting** with adjustments at each management level of the role hierarchy.
- **Territory Management** — assigns Accounts (and their Opportunities) to sales territories using rule-based or manual assignment, letting reps and managers see pipeline organized geographically or by named-account logic rather than strictly by ownership.

## 3. Lead Automation and Campaign Management

- **Lead Assignment Rules** — an ordered set of criteria-based rules that automatically route new/edited Leads to the correct owner or queue (e.g., by Lead Source, State, or Product Interest). Only one Lead Assignment Rule can be active at a time, but it can contain many ordered entries.
- **Campaigns** — track marketing initiatives (events, webinars, email blasts). **Campaign Members** link Leads or Contacts to a Campaign with a **Member Status** (Sent, Responded, Attended, etc.) that can be configured per campaign and used to calculate response rates.
- **Campaign Influence / Campaign ROI** — reports that tie Opportunity revenue back to the campaigns that influenced it, using standard fields like Amount, Budgeted Cost, and Expected Revenue on the Campaign object, or advanced multi-touch Campaign Influence models.
- Recall from Module 2: custom Lead fields need explicit **Map Lead Fields** configuration before conversion carries their values to the resulting Contact/Account/Opportunity.

## Common exam scenario patterns to recognize

- *"A custom Lead field's value disappears after conversion"* → missing entry in Map Lead Fields.
- *"Sales reps want to see which deals changed recently in list/Kanban view"* → Deal Change Highlights.
- *"Sales director wants to predict revenue across future quarters"* → Forecasting (Collaborative Forecasts), not just an Opportunity report.
- *"Reps need to prioritize which deals to work on first"* → Einstein Opportunity Scoring (deals) vs. Einstein Lead Scoring (leads) — pick the object that matches the scenario's noun.
- *"Give teammates credit for a shared deal based on effort"* → Opportunity **Splits**.
- *"New leads must route to the right rep based on territory/address"* → Lead Assignment Rule.

## Key takeaways for exam day

- Lead conversion requires explicit custom field mapping; standard fields map automatically.
- Distinguish Lead Scoring (which leads to call) from Opportunity Scoring (which deals to prioritize) from Forecasting (how much revenue is coming).
- Only one Lead Assignment Rule is active at a time, but it holds many ordered rule entries.
- Territory Management changes *how Opportunities/Accounts are organized and shared*, layered on top of (not replacing) the standard sharing model.
